title: Red Eyes, Gone! Discover the 10 Best Herbal Teas for Relief
intro_desc: Herbal teas for red eyes are a natural remedy that helps alleviate and treat red, itchy, and irritated eyes. They work by reducing inflammation, soothing the eyes, and promoting healing. The best herbal teas for red eyes include Euphrasia officinalis, Calendula officinalis, Symphytum officinale, Echinacea purpurea, Achillea millefolium, Ginkgo biloba, Hypericum perforatum, Aloe barbadensis, Chamomilla recutita, and Hamamelis virginiana. If left untreated, red eyes can cause discomfort, disrupt daily activities, and even impact work or school performance, leading to lost productivity and a reduced quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis tea is a natural remedy for red eyes. The tea is made from the leaves of the Euphrasia officinalis plant, which is also known as eye bright. This plant has been used for centuries to treat eye problems, including redness and irritation. The tea contains compounds that help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, which can cause redness. When you drink Euphrasia officinalis tea, these compounds are absorbed into your body and travel to your eyes, where they can start to work. The tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm your eyes. Additionally, Euphrasia officinalis tea can help to clear up any discharge or infection that may be causing your red eyes. This can help to clear up your vision and make your eyes feel more comfortable. Drinking Euphrasia officinalis tea regularly may also help to prevent red eyes from happening in the first place. The tea is also easy to make and can be drunk hot or cold, making it a convenient solution for people who experience red eyes.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for red eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and soothe the delicate skin around the eyes, making it a natural remedy to alleviate redness and discomfort.
Tannins, also known as St. John's Wort, are good for red eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease irritation in the eyes.
Kaempferol is good for red eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe irritation in the blood vessels around the eyes, which can cause redness.

remedies.image_alt: Euphrasia officinalis herbal teas for red eyes
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal tea, also known as St. John's Wort tea, start by gathering fresh or dried Hypericum perforatum flowers. If using fresh flowers, gently wash them in cold water to remove any dirt or impurities. Next, combine one teaspoon of the flowers with one cup of boiling water in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the flowers. If using dried flowers, steep one teaspoon of the dried flowers in one cup of boiling water for 3-5 minutes. Once the tea has cooled slightly, you can apply it to your closed eyes using cotton balls or pads soaked in the tea. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in Hypericum perforatum may help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, making it a popular natural remedy for red eyes. However, it's essential to note that Hypericum perforatum can interact with certain medications, so consult with a healthcare professional before using this herbal tea, especially if you have any underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tea is good for red eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties. When we get red eyes, it's often because of swelling or irritation. The anti-inflammatory properties in calendula tea help to reduce this swelling and calm down the irritation. This means that the tea can help to bring down the redness and make our eyes feel better. Calendula tea also has antiseptic properties, which help to prevent infection and promote healing. This can be especially helpful if our red eyes are caused by something like a cut or a scrape near the eye. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, calendula tea can help to speed up the recovery process and get our eyes back to normal.

Kaempferol is good for red eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and soothe irritated tissues in the eyes, making it a potential natural remedy for relieving redness and discomfort.

Saponins are good for red eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe irritation, making them a natural remedy for calming and comforting red, itchy, or bloodshot eyes.

remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal teas for red eyes
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal tea that helps with red eyes, start by gathering high-quality dried St. John's Wort flowers and leaves. Begin by boiling one cup of water and adding one teaspoon of the dried herb. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid has cooled to a comfortable temperature. Next, strain the liquid to remove the herb, and discard the solids. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure not to add too much, as this can change the flavor and medicinal properties of the tea. Once the tea has cooled, use it as needed to help soothe and reduce redness in the eyes. It's recommended to drink the tea in moderation, as St. John's Wort can interact with certain medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita tea is a popular remedy for red and irritated eyes. The tea contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink the tea, these compounds are absorbed into your body and can help reduce swelling and redness in your eyes. The tea also contains apigenin, a natural antihistamine that can help calm the nerves and reduce itchiness in the eyes. Additionally, Chamomilla recutita tea has soothing properties that can help calm the eyes and reduce stress. As a result, drinking the tea may help alleviate redness, itchiness, and irritation in the eyes, making it a good option for people who experience red eyes due to allergies, fatigue, or other irritants.

Quercetin is good for red eyes because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and reduce swelling in the blood vessels around the eyes, thus reducing redness and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal tea for red eyes, you will need dried St. John's Wort flowers and leaves. Start by boiling water in a teapot, then add one teaspoon of dried St. John's Wort flowers and leaves to the boiling water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to release their properties. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but it's best to drink it without any additives. The tea should be consumed 2-3 times a day for its potential benefits. It's essential to note that Hypericum perforatum may interact with certain medications, so consult a healthcare professional before using it, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are taking prescription medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a natural remedy that may help with red eyes. The plant contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and soothe the eyes. This can be especially helpful when you have conjunctivitis, a condition that causes red, itchy, and watery eyes. The tea may also help to calm the blood vessels in the eyes, which can become dilated and cause redness. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. Some people also use it to help reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes. When you drink the tea, the antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds can enter your body and help to reduce redness and swelling. This can leave your eyes feeling cooler and more comfortable.

remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for red eyes
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal tea that helps with red eyes, start by gathering 1-2 teaspoons of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers. You can dry the flowers yourself by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place, or purchase them from a health food store. Next, boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for a minute or two. Then, add the dried flowers to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. You can also add a sweetener like honey if you prefer. After the tea has steeped, strain it into a cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a tea strainer to remove the flowers. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe red, irritated eyes. It's also essential to note that you should consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or take prescription medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium tea, also known as yarrow tea, is a natural remedy for red eyes. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes. Yarrow tea contains flavonoids, a type of plant compound that fights off free radicals and prevents damage to the eyes. The tea also has antiseptic properties, which help to clean and protect the eyes from infections. When used as a compress or eye wash, yarrow tea can help to soothe and cool the eyes, reducing redness and puffiness. Additionally, yarrow tea can help to reduce the production of histamine, a chemical that causes blood vessels to swell and lead to redness and itching in the eyes. Drinking yarrow tea can also help to flush out toxins from the body, which can contribute to eye problems. Yarrow tea has been used for centuries to treat various eye problems, including conjunctivitis and pink eye.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal tea for red eyes, start by gathering dried St. John's Wort flowers, which are the common name for Hypericum perforatum. You can purchase dried flowers from a local health food store or online. Next, measure out one to two teaspoons of dried flowers for every eight ounces of water you plan to use. Pour boiling water over the dried flowers in a teapot or a mug, then let it steep for five to ten minutes. Strain the liquid into another container and discard the dried flowers. Allow the tea to cool down before using it. To use as an eye wash, dip a clean cloth in the cooled tea and gently apply it to your closed eyes. Alternatively, you can use the tea as a compress by soaking a cloth in the cooled tea and applying it to your eyes for five to ten minutes. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties in Hypericum perforatum may help reduce redness and soothe your eyes.

remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal teas for red eyes
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal tea that helps with red eyes, start by gathering fresh or dried Hypericum perforatum leaves. You can find this herb in health food stores or grow it in your garden. If using fresh leaves, wash them clean and pat them dry with a paper towel. Use one teaspoon of the leaves for every cup of boiling water. Place the leaves in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup and pour in the boiling water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth. Discard the solids and let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature for drinking. Once cooled, you can drink the tea as needed to help soothe and reduce redness in your eyes. It's recommended to drink 1-2 cups a day and to consult with a healthcare professional before using Hypericum perforatum for medicinal purposes.

supplementary_best: For red eyes, a combination of Euphrasia officinalis, Chamomilla recutita, and Eucalyptus globulus is effective. Euphrasia officinalis, also known as Eyebright, has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce puffiness and swelling. Chamomilla recutita, or German Chamomile, has a calming effect on the eyes, which helps to soothe irritation and discomfort. Eucalyptus globulus, or Eucalyptus, has decongestant properties that can help to clear mucus and reduce redness. When combined, these three herbs work together to provide relief from red eyes by reducing inflammation, soothing irritation, and clearing mucus.  This combination is particularly useful because it targets the root causes of red eyes, including inflammation, irritation, and congestion. By addressing these issues simultaneously, this blend can provide quick and effective relief from red eyes. The anti-inflammatory properties of Euphrasia officinalis, the calming effects of Chamomilla recutita, and the decongestant properties of Eucalyptus globulus make this combination a powerful remedy for red eyes.
supplementary_avoid: If you have red eyes, it's best to avoid certain herbal teas that can make the condition worse. One such tea is Sage (Salvia officinalis), which contains a compound called thujone. This compound can cause blood vessels to constrict, further irritating the eyes. Another tea to avoid is Valerian root (Valeriana officinalis), as it can cause drowsiness and dryness in the eyes. Black pepper (Piper nigrum) tea is also not recommended, as it can cause blood vessels to constrict and increase blood pressure, which can exacerbate red eyes. Additionally, tea made from the root of the Licorice plant (Glycyrrhiza glabra) should be avoided, as it can cause dryness and irritation in the eyes. Finally, tea made from the root of the Ashwagandha plant (Withania somnifera) should be avoided, as it can cause dryness and increased sensitivity in the eyes.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel can help soothe and calm red eyes. It has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease irritation. When applied to the affected area, it cools the eyes and provides relief from discomfort. The gel is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, which have antioxidant properties that protect the eyes from damage.  Cold compresses can also help reduce redness in the eyes. Applying a cold compress to the eyes for a few minutes can constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling. This can provide quick relief from red eyes caused by allergies, fatigue, or irritants. The cold temperature helps to numb the area, reducing discomfort and itching.  Cucumber slices can be used as a natural remedy for red eyes. When cooled in the refrigerator, cucumber slices can be placed over the eyes to provide relief. The cooling effect of the cucumber can reduce puffiness and ease irritation. Cucumbers are also rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe the eyes.  Eye drops made from natural ingredients like chamomile or calendula can help to calm and soothe red eyes. These drops work by reducing inflammation and itching, providing quick relief from redness. They can be used to treat a variety of eye issues, including dry eyes, allergies, and irritations.  Raw potatoes can be used as a natural remedy for red eyes. Grated raw potato can be applied to the eyes to reduce puffiness and ease irritation. The starch and anti-inflammatory compounds in potatoes help to soothe the eyes and provide relief from redness. This remedy can be used to treat a variety of eye issues, including allergies and irritations.  These natural remedies can provide relief from red eyes and help to soothe and calm the affected area. They work by reducing inflammation, itching, and swelling, and can be used to treat a variety of eye issues.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with red eyes may also experience other health issues. They can have dry or itchy eyes because of a lack of tears or an imbalance of the tear's pH level. Inflammation in the eyes can be caused by allergies, such as hay fever or dust mites, or by irritation from chemicals, smoke, or pollutants. Medicinal herbs like eyebright and goldenseal can help soothe and calm the eyes. Eyebright has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease redness, while goldenseal has antimicrobial properties that can fight off infections.  People with red eyes may also experience sinus problems like congestion, headaches, or facial pain. This is because the eyes and sinuses are connected through a network of small nerves and blood vessels. Herbs like eucalyptus and peppermint can help ease sinus congestion and reduce inflammation. Eucalyptus has decongestant properties that can open up airways and reduce mucus production, while peppermint has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the nasal passages and sinuses.  Furthermore, people with red eyes may also experience fatigue, blurred vision, or sensitivity to light. These symptoms can be caused by a lack of sleep, stress, or an underlying medical condition. Herbs like ginseng and ashwagandha can help alleviate fatigue and improve vision. Ginseng has adaptogenic properties that can help the body adapt to stress and increase energy levels, while ashwagandha has antioxidant properties that can protect the eyes from damage and promote clear vision.
